open_gaps: S0 remains unresolved: the immutable statement does not define the symbol ≫, and the candidate selects a preferred interpretation instead of treating the ambiguity as blocking.
G1: no uniform critical-scale PBD theorem with prime-power block sizes in [x,Bx] is proved.
G2: no uniform ITD(q+1,n;q) construction covering all required n≥q^2 is proved.
G3: no perfect-matching or absorption theorem for the orthogonal-array hypergraph at r=Theta(sqrt(n)) is proved.
G4: no universal infinite-family upper bound on max_L rho(L) strong enough to disprove the square-root assertion is proved.
Consequently, none of the disjunctive terminal routes S8, S12, S14, or S17 is completed.
counterexample_searches: The sparse-palette obstruction at v=1026 for K={32,64,243} and K={32,64,81,243} was arithmetically rechecked and is valid.
The parity proof that the cyclic Latin square of even order has no transversal was rechecked and is valid.
The parity obstruction for the stated translation-type construction on even orders was rechecked and is valid only as a construction-class obstruction.
The proposed C16 frame was checked directly against all ten coordinate pairs. Under the stated definition d_{i,h,j}=hC_{ij}, none of the ten frame multisets equals F_13 minus {0}.
For coordinate pair (0,1) in C16, anchor i=3 gives C_{31}-C_{30}=2-2=0, so zero occurs for all four h in H, immediately contradicting the required nonzero-difference partition.
The boundary case r=2 in C17 was checked: distinct vertices have codegree zero, not n^(r-3)=n^(-1).

material_errors.error: The candidate acknowledges that ≫ is undefined but then adopts the standard absolute interpretation. This violates the acceptance instruction that ambiguity is a blocking defect and does not resolve the exact immutable statement.
The candidate neither proves nor disproves the asserted square-root lower bound. Every terminal construction or obstruction is left conditional or open.

The displayed matrix does not satisfy C14 with the stated convention d_{i,h,j}=hC_{ij}. The listed difference triples instead correspond to transposed entries C_{ji}. As written, the construction has zero and repeated differences and therefore does not establish ITD(5,17;4).

The codegree formula is stated for unrestricted integers r,n but fails at r=2. It becomes correct only after adding the hypothesis r≥3, which the immutable boundary-case requirements do not permit the referee to insert silently.
The assertion that all 18 claims survived adversarial checking is false because C16 is invalid as written and C17 omits a necessary boundary hypothesis.

notes:
Most structural claims C2-C15 and C18 are sound or appropriately conditional, and C15's q=3 frame checks correctly. Nevertheless, the exact theorem is unresolved, the notation defect is not lawfully discharged, and one of the two advertised finite first-perturbation constructions is invalid under its own definitions. The candidate therefore cannot pass. Source candidate reviewed:
